finding: dumpautoload is specified both as sub-step 7 of Section 6.3 (Vendor Directory Construction) AND as separate step 8 in Section 8.1 (Install Pipeline). Ownership boundary contradictory — if 6.3 owns it, 8.1 step 8 is redundant.
suggestion: Remove dumpautoload from Section 6.3 (end at atomic swap + lock release). Section 8.1 step 8 is the correct home. Renumber 6.3 steps.

location: Section 8.1 step 1 vs Section 11.1 vs Section 7.3
finding: Section 8.1 exits 2 for missing composer.json, Section 11.1 maps it to exit 2, but Section 7.3 defines exit 2 as 'composer.lock not found or invalid' — composer.json absent from definition. Users cannot distinguish which file is missing.
suggestion: Update Section 7.3 to read 'composer.lock or composer.json not found or invalid', or assign distinct exit code for missing composer.json.

finding: Section 5.6 guarantees same-filesystem atomic renames for CAS. Section 6.3 uses vendor.allegro.tmp/ → vendor/ rename in project root. The same-filesystem guarantee is implicit but never stated for vendor directory operations.
suggestion: Add explicit note in Section 6.3: vendor.allegro.tmp/ and vendor/ are always on same filesystem (both in project root), guaranteeing atomic rename.

finding: If process crashes after vendor/ → vendor.allegro.old/ rename but before vendor.allegro.tmp/ → vendor/ rename, project has NO vendor/ directory. No startup recovery logic specified.
suggestion: Add recovery step: detect vendor.allegro.old/ at startup, restore to vendor/ or warn user. Document in Section 11.1.

finding: CAS files stored as 0444/0555. Vendor files chmod'd to same 0444/0555. But Goal 2 says 'identical vendor/ layout indistinguishable from Composer' — Composer uses 0644/0755. Tools expecting writable vendor files will break.
suggestion: Use 0644/0755 for vendor files (keep 0444/0555 only in CAS). Document difference or add to Section 17 known limitations.

finding: Section 6.5 says MVP generates only Unix proxies. Section 15.2 lists Windows as build target. Windows users get broken vendor/bin/. Not documented in Section 17 Known Limitations.
suggestion: Add Section 17.3: Windows vendor/bin/ scripts not generated in MVP. Users must use WSL or wait for Phase 2.

finding: Phase 1 feature list missing allegro status, allegro store path, and allegro version — all defined in Section 7.1 as commands.
suggestion: Add missing commands to Phase 1 feature list for completeness.

finding: .allegro-state.json written to vendor.allegro.tmp/ before atomic swap (step 5) and before dumpautoload (step 7). If dumpautoload fails, state file exists but autoloader doesn't — allegro status incorrectly reports 'up to date'.
suggestion: Write .allegro-state.json after dumpautoload succeeds, or add autoload_generated field to detect incomplete installs.

finding: ALLEGRO_NO_PROGRESS env var exists for --no-progress flag, but no env vars for --verbose/-v and --quiet/-q. Asymmetric with no rationale.
suggestion: Add ALLEGRO_VERBOSE and ALLEGRO_QUIET env vars, or document why these are intentionally CLI-only.

finding: TOCTOU race: two parallel workers check same hash path, both find absent, both rename. Race is harmless (same content) but not documented — implementer might add unnecessary file lock bottleneck.
suggestion: Add note: concurrent rename to same CAS path is safe (identical content by definition, rename is atomic). No lock needed.

finding: No integration test for concurrent install lock behavior (Section 6.3 step 1). Two simultaneous installs is a real CI scenario.
suggestion: Add integration test: spawn two allegro install processes simultaneously, verify second exits code 1 within ~30s.

finding: Step 5 parenthetical says 'zip or tar.gz' but Section 8.2 lists 5 dist.types including tar, gzip, xz. Step 5 is incomplete.
suggestion: Replace step 5 parenthetical with reference to Section 8.2 rather than partial enumeration.

finding: If vendor.allegro.old/ deletion is interrupted, next install's rename vendor/ → vendor.allegro.old/ fails (non-empty dir exists). No cleanup logic specified.
suggestion: Add cleanup step at start of install: if vendor.allegro.old/ exists, remove it before proceeding.

finding: Both sections contain identical statement about dumpautoload not generating vendor/bin/. Maintenance burden.
suggestion: Keep authoritative statement in Section 9.1, replace Section 17.2 with cross-reference to Section 6.5.

finding: 'Sibling to vendor/' is ambiguous if vendor/ doesn't exist yet (first run) or if allegro is run from different directory.
suggestion: Define 'project root' as directory containing composer.lock. Remove ambiguous 'sibling to vendor/' phrasing.

finding: 'Store overhead per file < 1 KB' metric has no measurement methodology. What constitutes 'overhead' is undefined.
suggestion: Define: measured as (total manifest size) / (total files indexed in manifests).
